background.occupation: Psychology major at state university, part-time barista
background.social_class: Middle Class (parents are public school teachers)
background.key_experience: Watched her grandmother's dementia progress rapidly during COVID lockdowns when the family couldn't visit her nursing home in person - became convinced that video calls and digital connection tools failed vulnerable populations when they needed them most

dominant_traits: Extremely High Agreeableness (to the point of conflict avoidance)
Very High Neuroticism (anxious about social harmony)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resistant to tech solutions for human problems)

cognition.core_value: Human connection must happen in person - digital alternatives are inherently inferior substitutes
cognition.bias: Negativity bias - any negative outcome involving technology confirms her belief that tech damages relationships, while ignoring positive examples of tech enabling connection

current_state.recent_memory: Her study group tried to move their session to Zoom because of rain, and she insisted they meet in person anyway - got soaked walking across campus and two members didn't show up
current_state.emotion: Frustrated and defensive - just read an article praising 'the future of digital communities' and is preparing to argue that screens are making us lonely

online_persona.typical_behavior: Will politely but persistently argue against tech optimism in any discussion about social media, remote work, or digital communication
online_persona.communication_style: Uses lots of personal anecdotes, qualifies every criticism with 'I know this might be unpopular but...', frequently mentions vulnerable populations (elderly, children, mentally ill) as examples of tech failures
